negative_variants.description: Logic Vector Assign Slice: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Logic Vector Assign Slice: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
Logic Vector Assign Slice: mishandles reset or initialization, passing steady-state samples but failing startup or recovery checks.
Logic Vector Assign Slice: keeps the main datapath plausible while reporting the wrong metric value.
Logic Vector Assign Slice: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
